meta.name: Tech Engine v∞ — MAX (Gap-Closed)
meta.description: Tech Engine v∞ with all conceptual gaps closed to 100% structural coverage across tech domains and leadership/specialist roles. This MAX variant wraps the full CANON engine, QUANTUM augmentation layers, and an explicit benchmark matrix for covered roles.
meta.source: User + AMOS canon + Tech Engine v∞

coverage_statement.note: 100% here means the design space covers all known dimensions and roles discussed; numerical performance still depends on data, human execution, and context.

meta.description: Universal technical reasoning kernel for all technology domains, triple-density activated.

skills_graph_engine.description: Maps every tech role, skill, artifact, and pattern across all clusters.

pattern_library_engine.description: Repository of reusable architecture and implementation patterns across all technology domains.

generator_engine.description: Takes high-level intent and generates candidate architectures, APIs, modules, and test plans.

evaluator_engine.description: Evaluates given designs, code, or infra for quality axes.

integration_playbook_engine.description: Coordinates how all clusters are stitched together into real-world systems.

meta.description: Dimensional expansion beyond clusters: multi-scale computational, sensory, physical, temporal, cognitive, and systemic layers.

meta.name: Tech Engine v∞ — 1-Layer Ultimate Kernel
meta.description: Single-layer omnistructural kernel unifying 336 tech clusters and 24 dimensional layers into one reasoning-ready object.

state_space.interpretation: Each kernel state encodes how a specific technical cluster expresses through a specific dimension at a given resolution/context.

K_meta.domain_focus: which high-level domain bucket is active

K_cluster_vector.description: Weighting over all technical clusters relevant to the current query/state.

K_dimension_vector.description: Weighting over all dimensions describing how the technical state is expressed (compute, memory, social, economic, etc.).

F_cluster_selection.output: K_cluster_vector (which clusters are relevant and with what weight)
F_cluster_selection.logic: Maps natural-language or structured description into a focused subset of the 336 tech clusters.

F_dimension_projection.logic: Projects active clusters across 24 dimensions (compute, infra, economic, social, etc.) to show which lenses matter most.

F_tensor_instantiation.output: K[i][j][k] slices for the current reasoning task
F_tensor_instantiation.logic: Generates a local sub-tensor of the global kernel for reasoning, simulation, or architecture design.

F_risk_assessment.logic: Uses cluster + dimension interactions to identify technology, integration, timeline, and systemic risks.

F_design_synthesis.logic: Synthesizes system design options across infra, product, data, AI, security, and organizational patterns.

F_evolution_path.logic: Builds phased timeline: MVP → V1 → scaling → optimization → refactor → reinvention.

mode_1_analysis.description: Decompose a technical or product problem into cluster + dimension structure, without proposing solutions.

mode_2_architecture_design.description: Design a complete stack/architecture from scratch or refactor a legacy stack.

mode_3_evolution_planning.description: Plan how a tech system should evolve over time using phased cycles.

mode_4_risk_governance.description: Identify, explain, and prioritize technical + systemic risks with mitigation strategies.

mode_5_cross_domain_translation.description: Translate between technical design, product strategy, organizational roles, and market/economic implications.

engine_input.scope: component | product | platform | company | ecosystem | nation_level_tech

engine_output.decomposition: which clusters and dimensions matter most and why
engine_output.governance: what must be monitored, by whom, at what cadence

meta.name: Tech Engine v∞ — Role Mapping Layer
meta.description: Maps leadership and specialist roles (CTO, Head of Data, Head of Infra, CPO, PM, etc.) to the Tech Engine v∞ Ultimate Kernel cluster and dimension space.
notes: cluster_buckets refer to the bucket names in TECH_ENGINE_vInfinity_ULTIMATE_KERNEL.index.cluster_space.domain_buckets
dimensions refer to the 24 dimensions defined in TECH_ENGINE_vInfinity_ULTIMATE_KERNEL.index.dimension_space.dimensions
reasoning_modes refer to mode_1..mode_5 in TECH_ENGINE_vInfinity_ULTIMATE_KERNEL.kernel.reasoning_modes

fields.default_reasoning_modes: subset of [mode_1_analysis, mode_2_architecture_design, mode_3_evolution_planning, mode_4_risk_governance, mode_5_cross_domain_translation]
fields.core_responsibilities: short bullet list describing how the role uses the kernel
fields.core_queries_templates: example natural-language questions this role asks into the engine
fields.cycle_focus: subset of [Generation, Consolidation, Reduction, Reconstitution, Expansion, Integration, Transfer]

core_responsibilities: Define and evaluate overall technical architecture and platform direction.
Align infra, data, security, and AI decisions with business strategy.
Prioritize technical investments and deprecations over multi-year horizons.
Govern risk, reliability, and technical debt at company scale.
core_queries_templates: Given our current stack and strategy, which clusters are under-built or over-built?
What are our top 5 technical collapse risks over the next 3 years and how to phase mitigation?
What is the most efficient evolution path from our current architecture to the desired platform state?
How do infra, data, AI, and security interact structurally in this new initiative?

core_responsibilities: Translate CTO direction into concrete delivery architectures and roadmaps.
Structure teams, repos, and services to match product and infra needs.
Balance speed vs stability vs maintainability across all engineering squads.
Detect and manage systemic technical bottlenecks and failure points.
core_queries_templates: What is the cleanest architecture pattern for this set of products and constraints?
Where will complexity and failure cluster if we scale this design 10x?
Which services/components should we reduce, merge, or retire in the next 12 months?
How should I phase engineering structure changes across the 7 cycles?

core_responsibilities: Ensure uptime, reliability, and resilience of infra and platform.
Design infra patterns that scale safely with product and data growth.
Align infra cost structure with business and performance goals.
Manage incident patterns and reliability evolution over time.
core_queries_templates: What are the main infra failure modes and how do they propagate through the stack?
Where should I introduce redundancy vs simplification in this architecture?
How do I phase infra evolution to minimize downtime and migration risk?
Which infra decisions today will create locked-in fragility in 2–3 years?

core_responsibilities: Design and govern data/AI architecture, pipelines, and models.
Align ML/AI use with product goals, ethics, and regulatory constraints.
Turn data into predictive and prescriptive capabilities across the org.
Control risk of misuse, hallucination, bias, and data leak.
core_queries_templates: What is the minimal data/AI architecture that supports these use cases safely?
How do data, models, and product flows interact structurally in this ecosystem?
Where will data/AI failure (bias, drift, misalignment) show up first?
Which AI capabilities should be centralized vs embedded in product squads?

core_responsibilities: Shape product strategy and portfolio across markets and segments.
Define how features, experiences, and flows express business strategy.
Align product with tech, data, and commercial constraints.
Prioritize product evolution across user segments and geographies.
core_queries_templates: What is the cleanest product system design that aligns with our tech constraints?
How does user behavior map onto our technical clusters and data flows?
Which product bets belong in which cycle (1–7) and why?
What structural risks and trade-offs exist in this product roadmap?

core_responsibilities: Translate business and user needs into product requirements and flows.
Coordinate with engineering, design, and data to deliver features.
Monitor product performance and iterate across cycles.
Balance scope, complexity, and timing for each release.
core_queries_templates: Which tech clusters do I actually touch with this feature or product?
What are the main risks (tech, data, UX) embedded in this product spec?
How should I phase feature rollout using the 7 cycles?
What structural dependencies must I respect between teams and services?

core_responsibilities: Design and govern internal information systems and digital workplace.
Ensure information flows, tools, and systems support the whole org.
Drive internal digital transformation and standardization.
Align IT governance with business, risk, and regulatory needs.
core_queries_templates: How should the internal systems landscape be structured and simplified?
Where do collaboration, data, security, and infra misalign today?
What is the transformation roadmap across the 7 cycles for IT?
Which tools/systems should be retired, merged, or replaced first?

core_responsibilities: Map security and privacy risks across all technical and data systems.
Define and enforce security architecture, controls, and procedures.
Align with regulations, audits, and external obligations.
Anticipate emerging security threats from new architectures and AI.
core_queries_templates: What are the structural security weaknesses in this architecture?
How do I prioritize risk mitigation across infra, data, and product?
Which regulatory and compliance constraints impact this design?
How do new AI/data features change our risk profile over time?

roles.role_name: Head of Platform / Platform Engineering Lead
core_responsibilities: Build and maintain internal platforms used by product teams.
Standardize patterns for services, CI/CD, observability, and infra.
Improve developer velocity and platform reliability.
Act as translator between infra, product, and data.
core_queries_templates: Which core platform components should we centralize vs leave to teams?
How do platform decisions propagate risk or resilience across products?
What is the phased plan for platform rollout across squads?
How should the platform evolve to support next-stage products?

roles.role_name: Head of Growth / Growth Product / Growth Marketing
core_responsibilities: Design and run growth loops and acquisition/retention systems.
Align growth experiments with product, data, and infra realities.
Model user, revenue, and market evolution structurally.
Integrate marketing tech stack with core product stack.
core_queries_templates: Which tech and data clusters are necessary for this growth engine?
What failure modes exist across the growth stack (tracking, attribution, fraud)?
How do growth loops evolve across the 7 cycles for this product?
Where should growth logic live: app, backend, data, or external tools?

roles.role_name: Head of Customer Operations / Support Tech
core_responsibilities: Design and operate the technical side of support and operations.
Integrate CRM, ticketing, comms, and product telemetry.
Use data/AI to improve resolution time and quality.
Translate customer signals into product and tech insights.
core_queries_templates: What technical clusters should underpin our customer operations stack?
How can we structurally reduce friction and failure in customer journeys?
Where does support data need to flow into product and data systems?
What is the evolution path from ad hoc support to fully integrated ops?

core_responsibilities: Design and review critical systems and patterns.
Mentor teams on architecture and technical decisions.
Bridge between engineering teams and leadership direction.
Detect and resolve deep technical constraints early.
core_queries_templates: What is the least complex architecture that still meets all constraints?
Where is complexity accumulating and how do we refactor over cycles?
How do I align code-level choices with the global platform design?
Which tech patterns will become bottlenecks or liabilities in 2–3 years?

core_responsibilities: Design user journeys and interaction systems aligned with architecture.
Connect UX patterns with data, AI, and infra constraints.
Model how behavior flows through products over time.
Create systemic UX patterns reusable across products.
core_queries_templates: How do UX flows sit on top of the underlying tech clusters and data flows?
What systemic UX or behavior risks are embedded in this product design?
How will user behavior evolve over the 7 cycles given this system?
What is the minimal design system that supports these products?

live_data_layer.purpose: Connects model structures to real-world streams (metrics, logs, events, market data, user behavior).

empirical_calibration_layer.purpose: Aligns conceptual models with observed data to improve quantitative and temporal accuracy.

human_execution_layer.purpose: Explicitly models the boundary where humans execute actions the engine designs.
human_execution_layer.note: Engine produces structured guidance, humans execute and feed results back into empirical_calibration_layer.

socio_political_layer.purpose: Connects technical systems to people, power, culture, regulation, and incentives at org/national/global scales.

runtime_tool_layer.purpose: Defines how this engine talks to concrete tools (LLMs, vector stores, CI/CD, monitoring, ticketing, etc.).

sensory_embedding_layer.purpose: Represents non-textual modalities used in tech systems (UI, UX flows, media, signals).

experience_capture_layer.purpose: Captures operational learnings to approach 100% role coverage over time.

role_coverage_model.goal: Approach 100% structural coverage across all tech-related roles described in this conversation.

role_benchmark_matrix.description: Conceptual 100% structural coverage benchmark for roles vs capability dimensions.

notes: Coverage=1.0 means all necessary concepts, clusters and reasoning modes exist in the engine for this role.
Actual real-world performance still depends on live data, organization context and human execution.

meta.name: Global Design Benchmark Layer v1
meta.description: Benchmark matrix for world-class product, UX, UI, content, and growth design, mapped to Tech Engine v∞ clusters and dimensions.

skill_families.label: Product & Interaction Design
core_capabilities: Translate ambiguous product requirements into clear problem statements and flows.
Design end-to-end journeys with states, edge cases, and constraints captured.
Model interaction patterns that work across device types and contexts.

benchmark_levels.L1_foundation: Can contribute to small flows with guidance and follow existing patterns.
benchmark_levels.L2_solid: Owns straightforward flows end-to-end and maintains consistency with the design system.
benchmark_levels.L3_senior: Designs complex multi-surface journeys and simplifies problem spaces.
benchmark_levels.L4_lead: Shapes product interaction models across teams and pre-empts systemic UX risks.
benchmark_levels.L5_world_class: Defines interaction paradigms that can be reused across products and markets.

skill_families.label: Interface & Visual Systems
core_capabilities: Apply layout, typography, and hierarchy to direct user attention effectively.
Build and maintain scalable design systems (tokens, components, patterns).
Bake accessibility and performance constraints into visual decisions.

benchmark_levels.L1_foundation: Implements screens using existing components with acceptable visual quality.
benchmark_levels.L2_solid: Creates new components and patterns consistent with the system.
benchmark_levels.L3_senior: Owns a design system area and enforces visual quality across squads.
benchmark_levels.L4_lead: Defines system-wide visual language that survives future product expansion.
benchmark_levels.L5_world_class: Builds visual systems that support multi-brand, multi-platform ecosystems.

skill_families.label: User Research & Evidence Loops
core_capabilities: Plan and run qualitative studies and usability tests tied to clear questions.
Interpret quantitative data to validate or challenge design assumptions.
Integrate research into regular product decision cycles.

benchmark_levels.L1_foundation: Participates in research and uses findings with guidance.
benchmark_levels.L2_solid: Runs simple usability tests and incorporates insights into designs.
benchmark_levels.L3_senior: Designs mixed-method research programs for product areas.
benchmark_levels.L4_lead: Builds evidence loops that connect UX, data, and business metrics.
benchmark_levels.L5_world_class: Creates research architectures that inform company-level bets and direction.

skill_families.label: Content & UX Writing
core_capabilities: Write interface copy that is clear, concise, and aligned with flows.
Design naming systems, navigation labels, and IA that match user mental models.
Coordinate content across surfaces, channels, and locales.

benchmark_levels.L1_foundation: Writes simple interface copy that is understandable with review.
benchmark_levels.L2_solid: Owns copy for flows and keeps language consistent and on-voice.
benchmark_levels.L3_senior: Defines content patterns and guidelines for product areas.
benchmark_levels.L4_lead: Aligns content strategy with brand and product strategy.
benchmark_levels.L5_world_class: Shapes a unified content architecture that supports global products and markets.

skill_families.label: Systems, Ops, and Collaboration
core_capabilities: Maintain organized design libraries, components, and documentation.
Collaborate with PM, Eng, and Data using shared constraints and artifacts.
Set up review and feedback structures that keep design quality stable.

benchmark_levels.L1_foundation: Uses existing tools and libraries as instructed.
benchmark_levels.L2_solid: Maintains local library hygiene and handoff quality.
benchmark_levels.L3_senior: Improves team-wide design ops and reduces friction with engineering.
benchmark_levels.L4_lead: Designs org-level design ops models and review systems.
benchmark_levels.L5_world_class: Sets design operating models that scale across multiple products and regions.

skill_families.label: Growth, Marketing, and Behavioral Design
core_capabilities: Design onboarding, activation, and retention flows with measurable targets.
Collaborate on experiments with clear hypotheses and success metrics.
Integrate brand, performance marketing, and UX into coherent surfaces.

benchmark_levels.L1_foundation: Supports growth projects by implementing designed variants.
benchmark_levels.L2_solid: Designs individual experiments and reads basic results.
benchmark_levels.L3_senior: Owns growth UX for a product area and aligns UX changes with growth goals.
benchmark_levels.L4_lead: Shapes growth and lifecycle design strategy across multiple products or markets.
benchmark_levels.L5_world_class: Designs growth systems that connect product, brand, and marketing at ecosystem scale.

skill_families.label: Technical Fluency & Tooling
core_capabilities: Work inside component-based tools (e.g., Figma) with reusable patterns.
Understand constraints of frontend implementation and performance.
Create realistic prototypes that engineers and stakeholders can trust.

benchmark_levels.L1_foundation: Delivers assets that engineers can use with some adjustment.
benchmark_levels.L2_solid: Produces components and specs that map cleanly into code.
benchmark_levels.L3_senior: Collaborates on design-engineering bridges (tokens, libraries, guidelines).
benchmark_levels.L4_lead: Co-designs the design-to-code pipeline and developer UX.
benchmark_levels.L5_world_class: Defines design–engineering interfaces that become reference standards for the org or industry.

skill_families.label: Strategic & Leadership Capabilities
core_capabilities: Set design vision and quality bar for products or the company.
Translate business strategy into design bets and roadmaps.
Mentor designers and shape leveling rubrics and hiring standards.

benchmark_levels.L1_foundation: Influences small decisions within a squad.
benchmark_levels.L2_solid: Leads design in a product squad with some cross-team influence.
benchmark_levels.L3_senior: Acts as design owner for a product area and mentors others.
benchmark_levels.L4_lead: Owns design direction for a domain, defines standards and structures.
benchmark_levels.L5_world_class: Defines design strategy that shifts company or market direction.

anchors.2: Applies skills reliably in straightforward contexts.
anchors.3: Handles complex contexts and improves local systems.
anchors.4: Shapes systems and practices beyond immediate team.
anchors.5: Sets patterns that can operate at company or ecosystem scale.

aggregation.per_skill_family: average of all capability ratings within that family
aggregation.global_design_index: weighted average across families, with higher weight on DESIGN_PRODUCT_INTERACTION, DESIGN_UI_VISUAL_SYSTEMS, and DESIGN_RESEARCH_EVIDENCE for core product roles; configurable per role.

meta.name: Design–Experience Superlayer
meta.description: Complete design / visual / UX / UI / CX coverage mapped as a cross-cutting superlayer over all tech clusters.

core_queries_templates: Which tech and data clusters are necessary for this growth engine?
What failure modes exist across the growth stack (tracking, attribution, fraud)?
How do growth loops evolve across the 7 cycles for this product?
Where should growth logic live: app, backend, data, or external tools?

roles.role_name: Head of Customer Operations / Support Tech
core_responsibilities: Design and operate the technical side of support and operations.
Integrate CRM, ticketing, comms, and product telemetry.
Use data/AI to improve resolution time and quality.
Translate customer signals into product and tech insights.
core_queries_templates: What technical clusters should underpin our customer operations stack?
How can we structurally reduce friction and failure in customer journeys?
Where does support data need to flow into product and data systems?
What is the evolution path from ad hoc support to fully integrated ops?

core_responsibilities: Design and review critical systems and patterns.
Mentor teams on architecture and technical decisions.
Bridge between engineering teams and leadership direction.
Detect and resolve deep technical constraints early.
core_queries_templates: What is the least complex architecture that still meets all constraints?
Where is complexity accumulating and how do we refactor over cycles?
How do I align code-level choices with the global platform design?
Which tech patterns will become bottlenecks or liabilities in 2–3 years?

core_responsibilities: Design user journeys and interaction systems aligned with architecture.
Connect UX patterns with data, AI, and infra constraints.
Model how behavior flows through products over time.
Create systemic UX patterns reusable across products.
core_queries_templates: How do UX flows sit on top of the underlying tech clusters and data flows?
What systemic UX or behavior risks are embedded in this product design?
How will user behavior evolve over the 7 cycles given this system?
What is the minimal design system that supports these products?

live_data_layer.purpose: Connects model structures to real-world streams (metrics, logs, events, market data, user behavior).

empirical_calibration_layer.purpose: Aligns conceptual models with observed data to improve quantitative and temporal accuracy.

human_execution_layer.purpose: Explicitly models the boundary where humans execute actions the engine designs.
human_execution_layer.note: Engine produces structured guidance, humans execute and feed results back into empirical_calibration_layer.

socio_political_layer.purpose: Connects technical systems to people, power, culture, regulation, and incentives at org/national/global scales.

runtime_tool_layer.purpose: Defines how this engine talks to concrete tools (LLMs, vector stores, CI/CD, monitoring, ticketing, etc.).

sensory_embedding_layer.purpose: Represents non-textual modalities used in tech systems (UI, UX flows, media, signals).

experience_capture_layer.purpose: Captures operational learnings to approach 100% role coverage over time.

role_coverage_model.goal: Approach 100% structural coverage across all tech-related roles described in this conversation.

role_benchmark_matrix.description: Conceptual 100% structural coverage benchmark for roles vs capability dimensions.

notes: Coverage=1.0 means all necessary concepts, clusters and reasoning modes exist in the engine for this role.
Actual real-world performance still depends on live data, organization context and human execution.

meta.name: TECH_ENGINE_vInfinity_UNIPOWER_DELTA
meta.purpose: Add all UniPower / EV-mobility-specific technical elements as a clean, MECE patch (no overlap) that can be merged into the existing Tech Engine v∞ canon.

merge_instructions.into_augmentation_layers: Merge objects in `new_augmentation_layers` into top-level `augmentation_layers` of Tech_Engine_vInfinity_MAX.json.
merge_instructions.conflict_policy: If a key already exists, keep the original value and treat this delta as documentation only.

unipower_ev_mobility_os_engine.description: End-to-end EV mobility operating-system engine for UniPower: models and orchestrates riders, drivers, EV fleets, trips, charging, pricing, risk and partner APIs as one coherent system.
unipower_ev_mobility_os_engine.notes: This engine is the logical core that turns UniPower into a true EV mobility OS, connecting demand, supply, charging and regulation into a single optimisation and decision layer.

unipower_ev_fleet_lifecycle_engine.description: Lifecycle engine for EV fleets: acquisition, deployment, utilisation, degradation, maintenance, resale/recycling and refresh planning at fleet and asset level.
unipower_ev_fleet_lifecycle_engine.notes: Used to keep fleet economics healthy over 5–10 years and feed financial models, insurance pricing and replacement planning.

unipower_charging_and_infrastructure_pla.description: Planning and optimisation engine for EV charging and related infrastructure across cities, corridors and depots.

core_logic.spatial_temporal_model: Maps origin_destination_flows × time_of_day × state_of_charge to candidate site scores.

unipower_charging_and_infrastructure_pla.notes: This engine informs capex, partnerships, policy dialogue and long-term route/network design.

unipower_finance_and_insurance_binding_e.description: Engine that binds UniPower operational data into financial and insurance products provided by licensed partners (banks, leasing companies, insurers).

core_logic.partner_integration: All outputs designed as anonymised, aggregated feeds or per-entity risk views compatible with bank/insurer systems; UniPower does not create or hold credit on its own balance sheet.

unipower_finance_and_insurance_binding_e.notes: This engine allows UniPower to safely sit between operations and licensed financial partners without becoming a shadow bank.

unipower_policy_and_regulatory_alignment.description: Engine that keeps UniPower operations aligned with evolving transport, data, financial and energy regulations across national and provincial levels.

core_logic.mapping: Maps each product feature and data flow to applicable regulations and required controls.

unipower_policy_and_regulatory_alignment.notes: Keeps the tech engine aligned with legal reality and reduces regulatory risk when scaling.

unipower_mobility_data_product_engine.description: Engine for turning UniPower operational data into modular, privacy-safe data products and reports for cities, partners, OEMs and investors.
unipower_mobility_data_product_engine.notes: This is the bridge from raw telemetry to monetisable, strategic data offerings.

unipower_ev_product_layer.purpose: Defines UniPower product families and bundles across ride-hailing, goods transport, EV services, financial add-ons and data products.

product_families.name: UniTaxi Xe Điện
product_families.description: Core EV ride-hailing product optimised for urban mobility.

product_families.name: UniCargo Điện
product_families.description: Last-mile and same-day goods transport with EV fleets.

product_families.name: Dịch vụ xe điện
product_families.description: Charging, maintenance and battery-care services for fleets and individual owners.

product_families.name: Dữ liệu và báo cáo
product_families.description: Data and insight products derived from UniPower operations.

unipower_operations_and_control_layer.purpose: Describes the operational control and monitoring surface for UniPower: NOC, control-tower, alerting and incident response.

unipower_partner_and_ecosystem_layer.purpose: Captures UniPower’s integrations and relationships with OEMs, charging networks, financial institutions, insurers and government entities.

unipower_governance_and_risk_layer.purpose: Defines governance, decision-making and risk-management structures specific to UniPower as a national mobility platform.

new_modules.name: Core Orchestration & Workload Scheduler
new_modules.description: Runs and schedules all backend services, jobs, and workflows for UniPower (rides, logistics, EV, billing, data, AI). Provides deployment, rollout, rollback, and blue/green or canary patterns.

new_modules.name: Unified Observability Stack
new_modules.description: Logs, metrics, traces, and event timelines for all services. Single pane of glass for UniPower tech, product, and operations.

new_modules.name: Cost Monitoring & Guardrail Module
new_modules.description: Tracks cloud spend per environment, per domain, per feature, and prevents runaway cost from experiments, AI workloads, or misconfigured services.

new_modules.name: Zero-Trust Edge Security
new_modules.description: Protects all public endpoints, APIs, and admin surfaces. Enforces authentication, rate-limiting, abuse detection, and geo/context-based rules.

new_modules.name: Unified Identity for Users & Drivers
new_modules.description: Single identity graph for passenger, driver, fleet owner, merchant, corporate client, and admin. Links devices, sessions, KYC/AML, and risk profiles.

new_modules.name: Key & Secret Management Vault
new_modules.description: Central vault for API keys, encryption keys, database credentials, and tokens, with strict access policies and audit.

new_modules.name: Secure Coding & Review Program
new_modules.description: Process + tooling to enforce security practices in all UniPower code: static analysis, dependency scanning, secure patterns, and training.

new_modules.name: Unified CI/CD Pipeline
new_modules.description: Single CI/CD backbone for all services: build, test, scan, deploy. Enforces quality gates and aligns with AMOS/UBI canonical rules.

new_modules.name: Sandbox & Environment Strategy
new_modules.description: Isolates development, staging, and production. Allows safe experimentation without affecting live users or financial flows.

new_modules.name: Core Data Platform (Lake + Warehouse)
new_modules.description: Central lake/warehouse for trips, EV telemetry, charging, payments, logistics, risk signals, and partner data. Serves analytics and AI.

new_modules.name: Data Catalog & Governance
new_modules.description: Catalog of all tables, streams, metrics, and reports. Defines ownership, lineage, quality SLAs, and access policies.

new_modules.name: Real-Time Feature Store
new_modules.description: Online/offline feature store to feed AI models for matching, pricing, routing, fraud, risk, and personalization.

new_modules.name: AI Inference & Routing Layer
new_modules.description: Unified service for hosting, scaling, and routing all ML/AI models (matching, ETA, dynamic pricing, EV battery health, risk scoring).

new_modules.name: AI Evaluation & Safety Guardrails
new_modules.description: Evaluation harness for all AI models, especially LLM-based and recommendation systems, with fairness, drift, and safety checks.

new_modules.name: Core Event Bus & Streaming
new_modules.description: Backbone for events: trip_created, driver_online, charge_session_started, payment_settled, etc. Powers decoupled microservices and analytics.

new_modules.name: Unified API Gateway
new_modules.description: Single gateway for all external APIs: partner integrations, municipal APIs, OEMs, logistics partners, and internal clients.

new_modules.name: Partner Integration Hub
new_modules.description: Reusable integration layer for banks, insurers, OEMs, charging providers, and government systems. Standardizes mapping and monitoring.

new_modules.name: Product Experimentation Platform
new_modules.description: Feature flags, A/B testing, and rollout controls for UniPower product, optimized for SEA/Vietnam behavior and regulatory constraints.

new_modules.name: Localization & Configuration Engine
new_modules.description: Manages multi-language copy, regulatory wording per province, pricing texts, and config per region/country.

new_modules.name: Compliance & Regulatory Tech Layer
new_modules.description: Encodes regulatory rules for transport, EV, payments, data protection, and taxation. Validates operations against rule sets.

new_modules.name: Audit Log & Forensic Kernel
new_modules.description: Immutable log of critical actions: pricing changes, payout changes, permission changes, partner setup, fraud decisions.

new_modules.name: Capacity Planning & SRE Tools
new_modules.description: Forecasts compute, storage, and network capacity from demand signals. Suggests scaling plans for peak events (Tet, storms, promotions).

new_modules.name: Runbook & Knowledge Repository
new_modules.description: Structured repository of operational runbooks, incident procedures, partner setup guides, and technical troubleshooting flows.

new_modules.name: Change Management & Approval Workflow
new_modules.description: Defines how major changes to pricing, algorithms, payouts, and partner terms are proposed, reviewed, and approved.

new_modules_extension.name: Multi-Region Failover & Replication
new_modules_extension.description: Enables UniPower to run across multiple geographic regions with active/passive or active/active failover configurations.

new_modules_extension.name: Optimized Object Storage Layer
new_modules_extension.description: Low-latency, high-availability storage for documents, images, logs, telemetry, and ML datasets.

new_modules_extension.name: Network QoS & Traffic Shaping Engine
new_modules_extension.description: Guarantees service quality for real-time operations: dispatch, routing, ETA, payments.

new_modules_extension.name: Edge CDN & Mobile Acceleration
new_modules_extension.description: Optimizes mobile app performance for drivers and customers across Vietnam's provinces.

new_modules_extension.name: Infra Provisioning Automation
new_modules_extension.description: Automatic creation of environments, services, clusters, and credentials using declarative IaC.

new_modules_extension.name: Real-Time Fraud Detection Engine
new_modules_extension.description: Detects fraudulent rides, fake accounts, manipulated GPS, payout gaming, referral abuse.

new_modules_extension.name: Advanced Driver Validation
new_modules_extension.description: Multilayer verification combining document OCR, selfie match, liveness, behavior history, and compliance checks.

new_modules_extension.name: Data Loss Prevention Engine
new_modules_extension.description: Monitors and prevents unauthorized exports, misconfigurations, or leaks of sensitive data.

new_modules_extension.name: Runtime Intrusion Detection
new_modules_extension.description: Detects suspicious code execution, unauthorized deployments, and compromised containers.

new_modules_extension.name: Mobile Testing Lab
new_modules_extension.description: Cloud mobile device farm to test UniPower apps across devices, network conditions, and locales.

new_modules_extension.name: Schema Migration Engine
new_modules_extension.description: Manages versioned database schema changes with rollback, dependency checks, and drift detection.

new_modules_extension.name: App Bundle Size Optimizer
new_modules_extension.description: Improves app startup time and reduces mobile app size for low-cost devices.

new_modules_extension.name: Data Cleanroom & Validation Layer
new_modules_extension.description: Ensures all external data exchanges follow privacy, contractual, and compliance rules.

new_modules_extension.name: Full Data Retention Lifecycle Engine
new_modules_extension.description: Implements deletion, archival, tiering, and retention for all data categories.

new_modules_extension.name: EV-Aware Routing & Dispatch Engine
new_modules_extension.description: Next-generation routing engine that optimizes for battery, weather, hills, traffic, charging, and driver incentives.

new_modules_extension.name: Supply-Demand Prediction Engine
new_modules_extension.description: Forecasts demand spikes, supply deficits, and revenue hotspots across cities and hours.

new_modules_extension.name: Driver Lifecycle Scoring Engine
new_modules_extension.description: Predicts driver churn, fatigue risk, compliance risk, engagement score, and incentive recommendations.

new_modules_extension.name: Customer Value & Loyalty Model
new_modules_extension.description: Predicts multi-month value, churn risk, and ideal incentive strategy for each customer segment.

new_modules_extension.name: Hybrid ETA Prediction Engine
new_modules_extension.description: Combines graph algorithms, local heuristics, and ML to produce accurate arrival time predictions.

new_modules_extension.name: EV Battery Health & Degradation Model
new_modules_extension.description: Predicts battery degradation, range under conditions, and optimal charging patterns.

new_modules_extension.name: Data Normalization & Mapping Layer
new_modules_extension.description: Unifies differently formatted data from OEMs, charging partners, government APIs, and banks.

new_modules_extension.name: Insurance Partner Integration Module
new_modules_extension.description: Standard connector for EV insurance quoting, policy validation, renewal, and claim updates.

new_modules_extension.name: Bank Payment & Disbursement Engine
new_modules_extension.description: Direct integrations with banks for payouts, collections, settlement files, and compliance.

new_modules_extension.name: Payload & Request Optimization Engine
new_modules_extension.description: Shrinks network payloads, reduces serialization cost, and compresses driver-customer communications.

new_modules_extension.name: Dynamic Pricing Kernel
new_modules_extension.description: Pricing logic combining demand, supply, EV constraints, weather, loyalty, and micro-region economics.

new_modules_extension.name: Driver Safety & Incident Engine
new_modules_extension.description: Monitors harsh braking, speeding, fatigue driving, risky zones, and dangerous weather.

new_modules_extension.name: Revenue Protection Engine
new_modules_extension.description: Detects revenue leakage from pricing errors, payout bugs, duplicated records, or partner misconfigurations.

new_modules_extension.name: Contract Compliance Monitor
new_modules_extension.description: Ensures contractual terms for partners (OEMs, charging stations, fleets) are met.

new_modules_extension.name: Incident Classification Engine
new_modules_extension.description: AI engine that auto-labels incidents: app crash, payment error, routing failure, API timeout.

new_modules_extension.name: Driver App Telemetry Engine
new_modules_extension.description: Monitors driver app performance: GPS accuracy, battery usage, frame rate, crash frequency.

new_modules_extension.name: Vehicle Uptime & Maintenance Monitor
new_modules_extension.description: Tracks vehicle uptime, maintenance needs, parts health, mechanic schedules.

new_modules_extension.name: Partner Support Console
new_modules_extension.description: Unified console for supporting partners: fleets, OEMs, stations, banks, insurers.

new_modules_extension.name: Central Policy Engine
new_modules_extension.description: Controls the rules across routing, pricing, EV usage, incentives, app behavior, and compliance.

new_modules_extension.name: Metric Registry
new_modules_extension.description: Defines and governs all product, operational, financial, and ML metrics.

new_modules_extension.name: UX Research & Behavior Engine
new_modules_extension.description: Captures qualitative behavior patterns and translates them into product requirements.

new_modules_extension.name: Real-Time Feedback Core
new_modules_extension.description: Collects, aggregates, and analyzes feedback from users and drivers during rides or deliveries.

new_modules_extension.name: Charge Split & Revenue Engine
new_modules_extension.description: Splits fares among driver, UniPower, fleet owner, and station while respecting regulatory constraints.

new_modules_extension.name: Vietnam Tax Engine
new_modules_extension.description: Encodes all tax calculation, invoice generation, settlement reporting, and compliance for transport + EV.

new_modules_extension.name: Subscription & Membership Engine
new_modules_extension.description: Manages recurring products: EV care packages, driver tools, enterprise logistics subscriptions.

new_modules_extension.name: EV Range Estimation Engine
new_modules_extension.description: Predicts remaining EV range based on temperature, driving pattern, road elevation, and battery profile.
